title: The envelope you didn't expect
subtitle: A short visualisation for receiving surprise money with ease.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation practice from Day 33 of the MONEY program. The scene: an unexpected envelope arriving, and remaining calm when you open it.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for receiving unexpected income without the checking-for-the-catch reflex.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 33 of the MONEY program. An envelope in the post that you did not expect, containing money.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take a slow breath in and let it go.
Picture yourself at the letterbox. There is post. Most of it is the usual: bills, a catalogue, something from the council. One envelope is different. Handwritten address. Your name.

You open it. Inside: a cheque, a bank transfer letter, a note with a payment. The source is real but unexpected: a refund you'd forgotten about, a gift from a relative, a payment for work you did months ago and gave up waiting for. Pick whichever version feels true.
The number is significant. Not enormous: significant. Enough to notice.
Now notice how you hold it. Do you immediately check for the catch? Do you feel relief? Do you feel surprised that it feels ordinary? Stay with whatever comes.
Picture yourself tucking the envelope under your arm and going inside. It's yours. No catch. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation for receiving unexpected income.
